QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_31

question.text: As per SEBI's categorization of debt mutual fund schemes, a Short Duration Fund has a Macaulay duration of __________.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI categorizes debt fund schemes by Macaulay duration: Overnight (1 day), Liquid (up to 91 days), Ultra Short Duration (3-6 months), Low Duration (6-12 months), Short Duration (1-3 years), Medium Duration (3-4 years), Medium to Long Duration (4-7 years), and Long Duration (greater than 7 years). Short Duration Fund falls in the 1 to 3 years bracket.
**The Trap:** 3-6 months is Ultra Short Duration, 6-12 months is Low Duration, and 3-4 years is Medium Duration. These adjacent categories are commonly confused. The naming can be misleading since "short" intuitively might suggest a shorter duration than 1-3 years.
**Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI's debt fund categorization by Macaulay duration is a core topic in NISM V-B. Memorizing the full duration spectrum is essential.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short Duration = 1-3 years Macaulay duration. The full sequence: Overnight < Liquid < Ultra Short < Low < Short < Medium < Medium-Long < Long.
